SnowDistribution: Cloudy on the tops with some bright spells. Winds approx 40 - 50 mph making progress very difficult and strenuous. Diminishing snowpack at lower elevations, good cover above 800 metres.
Comment: Freezing level rising above the summits through Monday evening allowing for a continuing thaw.
WeatherInfluences: The freezing level will be 850 metres initially then rising steadily to 1200 metres by the end of the forecasting period. Precipitation throughout much of the time, with a brief respite on Monday afternoon. At first this will fall as snow to around 800 metres but latterly become confined to the highest summits. Very strong winds mostly from the West-South-West will decrease through the daytime to become fresh.
SnowStability: Unstable windslab will continue to build primarily on North to East aspects above 900 metres. These deposits will be mostly confined to wind-sheltered locations such as gully tops and coire rims and will start to consolidate as the freezing level rises towards the end of the forecasting period. Fragile cornices will develop in the same areas. Elsewhere the snowpack will be mostly firm and stable, with the surface softening later in the period. The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: Rain at all levels overnight, followed by light snowfall above 950 metres during the day. Gale force West-South-Westerly winds with powerful gusts. The freezing level was around 1000 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: Unstable windslab is developing in wind sheltered North to East aspects above 1000 metres with areas most affected being gully tops and coire rims. Elsewhere the old snowpack is continuing to consolidate. The snow surface is firm above 1000 metres, becoming increasingly soft and moist at lower elevations. The avalanche hazard is Moderate.

SnowDistribution: Moist snow at most elevations, firmer at higher elevations.  Cloudy on the tops with some bright spells on lower slopes. Strong steady winds above 1000m gusty in coires.
Comment: A consolidating trend as temperatures fluctuate. Some windslab development is expected with modest snow quantities. However, be alert to the possibility of greater quantities of snow, which will increase the depth of windslab and augment the \'Wind Slab Avalanche Problem).
WeatherInfluences: Precipitation is expected through much of the period. This will fall as snow initially above around 900 metres overnight, and confined to above 1000 metres during the day. Winds will mostly be from the South-West veering to the West, generally very strong, but dropping to fresh later. The freezing level will gradually rise from 850 metres to 1100 metres by the end of the period.
SnowStability: Moderately bonded windslab will be deposited on North-West, through North to East aspects above 900 metres. Areas most affected will be steep wind sheltered locations such as coire rims and gully tops, where deposits will be mostly avoidable. Isolated wet snow instabilities will develop later as temperatures rise. Fresh unstable cornices will form around gully tops and the edge of plateaus as the day progresses. The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: Overnight rain was followed by sleet and then snow above 950 metres. Winds were very strong and generally from the West-South-West. The freezing level was around 1000 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: Following a period of instability early in the period, a more favourable situation was observed during the day. The snowpack was moist at all levels, with a firmer snow surface above 950 metres. Isolated shallow windslab deposits started to develop on North-West through North to East aspects above 950 metres. Extensive snow loss has occurred with the greatest extents above 800 metres. The avalanche hazard is Low.

SnowDistribution: Mainly wet snowpack, with some firmer hard conditions on the summits.  Cloudy on the tops with some bright spells.  Winds 20 -30 mph with stronger gusts.
Comment:
There is some uncertainty regards forecast snow amounts through Sunday evening into Monday morning. If we get the upper end of forecast amounts, then some fresh drifts may be larger than forecast, but this will only affect the highest summits, above 900 metres.
Mild conditions are forecast for Tuesday onwards, with perhaps a return to cooler summit temperatures by next weekend.
WeatherInfluences: Frequent showers through this evening, and into Monday morning, will give some snow above 850 metres. In the afternoon it will become drier. Strong to very strong, mainly South-Westerly winds, will moderate to fresh by the end of the period. The freezing level will slowly rise from 900 metres to 1100 metres through the day.
SnowStability: There will be some fresh wet snow deposits above 850 metres, on North to Westerly aspects. These are not expected to be significant, and should be shallow and well bonded, but there is some slight uncertainty regards snowfall amounts. The existing snowpack will continue to consolidate and thaw, but remain firm at higher elevations, with good stability at all levels. The avalanche hazard will be Low.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: There were frequent squally showers throughout the day. These were of sleet and snow above 900 metres . Winds were strong to very strong South-Westerly on the summits and the freezing level was around 950 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: At all levels, consolidation of the snowpack continues, with thawing and some depletion at lower levels. Greatest snow accumulations are found above 800 metres, on North to Easterly aspects. Icy patches, and firmer snow exist at higher elevations. The avalanche hazard is Low

SnowDistribution: Wet snowpack below 1100 metres. Poor visibility above 800 metres. Winds 30 - 40 mph, progress difficult and tiring.
Comment: The freezing level will rise above all summits on Monday night.
WeatherInfluences: Gale force South-Westerly winds will ease in the afternoon. Showers will fall as snow above around 800 metres. The freezing level will slowly rise from 900 to 1100 metres.
SnowStability: Fresh unstable windslab will continue to develop with greatest accumulations on North to East aspects above 1000 metres. Fragile cornices will develop above these aspects. Windward aspects will have good stability. The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: There were gale force South-Westerly winds and some heavy showers falling as snow above 850 metres. The freezing level was around 1050 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: Areas of unstable windslab were developing mainly on North to East aspects above 1100 metres. Elsewhere the snowpack is generally stable. Cornices are prone to collapse. The avalanche hazard is Moderate.

SnowDistribution: Thaw conditions at most elevations.  Cloudy on the tops with some bright spells, mostly in the morning. Winds 30 -40 mph.
Comment: Precipitation will be showers with some uncertainty in snowfall amounts. If less snowfall occurs before the freezing level rises the hazard may be lower.
WeatherInfluences: Showers throughout the forecast period will initially be snow above around 950 metres gradually turning to rain at higher levels as the freezing level rises above all summits by the end of the afternoon. Strong West-South-Westerly winds will decrease in strength through the day.
SnowStability: The existing snowpack will continue to consolidate and stability here will be good. Localised deposits of moderately bonded windslab will develop above 1000 metres and gradually consolidate as the freezing level rises through the day. Areas most affected will be wind sheltered locations such as gullies and coire rims on North to East aspects above 1000 metres. The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: Showers throughout the day became heavier in the afternoon and were rain below around 900 metres, changing between rain and wet snow at higher elevations. The freezing level was around 1050 metres with strong South-Westerly winds.
ObservedSnowStability: The existing snowpack continues to thaw and consolidate, stability here is good. New wet snow deposits on the highest summits are shallow and reasonably well bonded. The avalanche hazard is Low.

SnowDistribution: Good snow cover from 600m but exposed locations becoming patchy. No visibility above 900m first thing, good visibility later. Winds 30-40 mph.
Comment: Outlook is mild for the start of the week. Risk of large cornice collapse if the thaw becomes well established.
WeatherInfluences: Light snowfall overnight above 800 metres. By late morning precipitation will only fall as light snowfall above 1050 metres. Mainly dry afternoon. Winds will be variable, South-West to Westerly, strong throughout. 800 metre freezing level overnight, rising to 1200 metres by the end of the period.
SnowStability: Surface layers will gradually firm up above 800 metres overnight, softening again later as the freezing level rises to the summits. Mostly well bonded, stable conditions. Shallow windslab will start to form in wind sheltered locations on North through East to South-East aspects, initially above 800m, later only above 1000m. New snow amounts are not expected to be significant and will start to consolidate as the freezing level rises. Cornices will be present. The avalanche hazard will be Low.
ObservedWeatherInfluences: A cloudy morning with very light precipitation falling as snow above 900 metres, followed by a drier and brighter afternoon. Winds were gale force West, then West-South-Westerly. Freezing level fluctuated around 950 metres.
ObservedSnowStability: The snow has continued to consolidate after the recent milder conditions and is now generally well bonded. Surface layers are starting to firm up above 1000 metres, progressively softer and wetter snow at increasingly lower altitudes. Some large cornices remain present. The avalanche hazard is Low.
